task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a dashboard named 'SRE Incident Response'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'SRE Incident Response' in the dashboard title area, header, or breadcrumb navigation of the Graphite web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the graph is titled 'EC2 Fleet CPU Correlation'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'EC2 Fleet CPU Correlation' displayed as the title directly above or within the graph panel.

task_completion.description: Verify the three specific EC2 instance CPU metrics are added to the graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the graph legend or query editor for 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization', 'servers.ec2_instance_2.cpu.utilization', and 'servers.ec2_instance_3.cpu.cloudwatch_utilization'.

task_completion.description: Verify the movingAverage function with a window of 10 is applied to the individual metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the graph legend or query editor for the 'movingAverage' function wrapping the individual metrics, specifically with the parameter '10' (e.g., movingAverage(..., 10)).

task_completion.description: Verify a fleet-wide average baseline is included using averageSeries
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the graph legend or query editor for an 'averageSeries' function that wraps or targets all three of the EC2 CPU metrics to create a single baseline curve.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Graphite web interface to construct the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Graphite UI, query builder, or dashboard editor rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the graph actually renders data curves
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual plotted lines on the graph panel, indicating genuine interaction with the time-series data backend rather than a static mock or empty state.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply edit the result JSON file directly
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no screenshots of a text editor (like nano or vim) open with the raw JSON dashboard definition being manually typed or pasted.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'SRE Incident Response'. The expected graph title is exactly 'EC2 Fleet CPU Correlation'. The graph must contain the metrics 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization', 'servers.ec2_instance_2.cpu.utilization', and 'servers.ec2_instance_3.cpu.cloudwatch_utilization'. Each of these three metrics must be wrapped in the 'movingAverage' function with a window parameter of 10. There must also be an 'averageSeries' function applied across all three metrics to serve as a baseline.
